page.title: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia: 112 alterations and 635 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: A systematic review of the literature showed that Permixon was effective for relieving urinary symptoms due to prostate enlargement.
A 750mg dose of flower pollen (Cernilton) improves symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia, by improving postvoid residual urine, urine retention and the rate of surgery.
A comparative study on the efficacy of phytosterol-enriched and conventional saw palmetto oil in mitigating benign prostate hyperplasia and androgen deficiency.
A low-fat, low-fiber diet and daily exercise lowers insulin and reduces growth of prostate cells.
A swedish flower pollen extract has therapeutic value in treating chronic prostatitis and prostatic adenoma.
An extract of saw palmetto compares favorably with the drug tamsulosin in the treatment of benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Dietary flaxseed lignan improves lower urinary tract symptoms in benign prostatic hyperplasia subjects as well as drug therapy.
Dietary intake of isoflavone is inversely associated with the risk of lower urinary tract symptoms.
Effectiveness and safety of electroacupuncture and its cotreatment with electronic moxibustion in the treatment of patients with moderate benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Effects of L-carnitine and coenzyme Q10 supplementation on lower urinary tract symptoms in men with benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Effects of lycopene-enriched, organic, extra virgin olive oil on benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Efficacy and safety of a flaxseed hull extract in the symptomatic management of benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Electrocupuncture has a significant therapeutic effect for treatment of benign hyperplasia of prostate.
Evaluation of efficacy of cholecalciferol and silymarin in improving lower urinary tract symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Flaxseed supplementation and a low-fat diet reduces proliferation of benign prostatic epithelium and PSA.
Lycopene inhibits disease progression in patients with benign prostate hyperplasia.
Nettles has beneficial effects in the treatment of Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia.
Oil-free hydroethanolic pumpkin seed extract could be beneficial for benign prostatic hyperplasia related symptoms.
Oral combination of pumpkin seed extract, soy germ isoflavonoids, and cranberry could be beneficial for benign prostate hyperplasia patients.
Peripubertal aromatase inhibition in male rats has adverse long-term effects on bone strength and growth and induces prostatic hyperplasia.
Pumpkin seed oil relieved benign prostatic hyperplasia symptoms with no side effects
Pumpkin seed oil (prostafit) is effective and a safe alternative treatment in benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Pumpkin seed oil and saw palmetto oil may be effective as complementary and alternative medicine treatments for benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Pumpkin seed oil versus tamsulosin for benign prostatic hyperplasia symptom relief.
Pycnogenol may be an important option for self-management of benign prostatic hyperthrophy in otherwise healthy men.
Pygeum Africanum reduces prostatic hyperplasia effectively and safely. - Article 1
Reishi (Ganoderma lucidum) safely improves lower urinary tract symptoms in men with benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Royal jelly could be used to reduce PSA levels in blood.
Saw palmetto compares favorably with the drug Flomax (tamsulosin) to alleviate benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH).
Serenoa repens extract was effective, safe, and well-tolerated for lower urinary tract symptoms/benign prostatic hyperplasia.
Serenoa repens seems to reduce prostatic inflammation in terms of histological and immunohistochemical parameters.
Serum 25-OH D levels are inversely associated with overall prostate volume and enlarged prostate gland.
The effect of melatonin on improving the benign prostatic hyperplasia urinary symptoms.
The results of our study support the effect of vitamin D in reducing prostate volume and PSA levels, and in improving BPH symptoms.
Therapeutic use of pulsed electromagnetic field therapy reduces prostate volume and lower urinary tract symptoms in benign prostatic hyperplasia.
There is a significantly reduced omega-3/omega-6 polyunsaturated fatty acid ratio in the prostate cancer patient as opposed to the benign hyperplasia group.
This study of Serenoa repens extract indicates the efficiency of this phytotherapeutic agent in patients with benign prostate hyperplasia.
Tomato paste consumption reduces PSA levels in patients with benign prostate hyperplasia.
Urtica dioica root extract consumption for 12 weeks among BPH patients had clinically significant effects on IPSS, serum hs-CRP, MDA and SOD activity.
Vitamin D deficiency may be a marker of benign prostatic hyperplasia.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive natural history; symptoms worsen in 50-60% over 5 years without treatment; spontaneous improvement in 20-30% (particularly mild symptoms); acute urinary retention risk ~1-2%/year
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Combination alpha-blocker + 5-ARI (tamsulosin + dutasteride): reduces prostate volume 25-30%, acute urinary retention risk 67% (CombAT trial); minimally invasive procedures (Rezum, Urolift, Aquablation): IPSS improvement 50-60%, preserving ejaculatory function better than TURP; TURP: gold standard, 70-80% symptom improvement
remission.gap_size: Moderate — treatment is effective but procedure uptake and pharmacotherapy titration is suboptimal; many patients overtreated with TURP when less invasive options appropriate
remission.barrier_detail: Alpha-blockers provide symptomatic relief but do not address prostate volume in large glands (5-ARI needed); TURP remains widely used when Rezum/Urolift preserve sexual function with similar efficacy; pharmacotherapy stepped-up without referral in cases where early MIS procedure would preserve more function
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — Aquablation FDA approved; 2019 Rezum (water vapour therapy) established; 2016 Urolift established; 5-ARI added to alpha-blocker standard since CombAT trial 2008; alpha-blockers first-line since 1990s; TURP gold standard since 1960s
remission.outdated_treatments: Open prostatectomy for glands <100g (TURP or HoLEP preferable); terazosin/doxazosin as first-line alpha-blockers (uroselective agents tamsulosin/silodosin have better BP side effect profile); saw palmetto (multiple large RCTs null including STEP and CAMUS trials — yet widely used)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Saw palmetto (STEP and CAMUS trials both null but widely used); pumpkin seed oil (very limited trial data); dietary: lycopene, zinc; pelvic floor exercises for mild overactive bladder component; beta-3 agonist (mirabegron) for mixed OAB/BPH symptoms
remission.frontier_signal: Convective water vapour ablation (Rezum) expanding; high-frequency focused ultrasound (HIFU) for BPH; intraprostate botulinum toxin injection (Phase 2 data); prostate arterial embolisation (PAE) for large glands; 5G/robotic-assisted Aquablation
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
